input_candidates.truth_value: This review covers the physical metallurgy, processing strategies, strengthening mechanisms and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C low density steels for automotive applications, and suggests future avenues.

input_candidates.truth_value: Young's modulus of low density steels decreases by 2-2.5% per 1 wt% Al addition and is significantly lower than that of conventional HSLA steels (~160-175 GPa vs 210 GPa).

input_candidates.truth_value: Addition of Al suppresses cementite precipitation and promotes short-range ordering (SRO) and kappa'-carbide, which leads to glide plane softening and planar slip.

input_candidates.truth_value: Three new deformation mechanisms are introduced for high-SFE austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steels: microband induced plasticity (MBIP), dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR), and shear band induced plasticity (SIP).

input_candidates.truth_value: Effective Al composition range for austenitic low-density steels with best mechanical properties is 7-10 wt% Al, with typical compositions: 15-30% Mn, 7-10% Al, 0.7-1.2% C.
